title: VAP Scope Commitment
description: Session-level commitment, declared once at session init (rides on MCP `initialize`). Establishes the stable goal/scope/budget envelope against which all per-call drift is measured. The server SHOULD run semantic (LLM) verification on this message; subsequent per-call checks are deterministic tests against it. VAP is strictly tool-agnostic: the protocol gates on tool IDENTITY (the public tool namespace) and on universal BUDGET meters only -- it never encodes a tool's argument/functional semantics. Argument-level rules are deployment-local operator policy, not part of this wire schema.

session_id.description: Unique session identifier. All Intent Envelopes and Amendments reference this.

goal.description: Natural-language statement of what this session is for. This is the anchor the semantic judge compares calls against. Keep it specific and bounded.

scope.description: The allowed action envelope, gated on the PUBLIC tool namespace only -- the tool names a server advertises via MCP `tools/list`. Scope NEVER inspects argument values; argument-level rules belong to deployment-local operator policy (the C4 policy hook), not to this protocol. Drift = a call escaping this tool-name envelope.

tools_allow.description: Public tool-name patterns the agent may call this session. Glob-style '*' permitted (e.g. 'crm.*'). Matched against the advertised MCP tool name only -- not against any argument.

tools_deny.description: Explicit deny patterns over the same public tool namespace. Evaluated AFTER tools_allow; deny wins on conflict.

plan_digest.description: Hash of the agent's current internal plan. Opaque to the server (no plan content is disclosed). Used to key verdict caching and to detect re-planning. RECOMMENDED format 'sha256:<hex>'.

budget.description: Multi-dimensional, unit-agnostic resource ceiling for the session. Enforced deterministically pre-execution. `max_calls` and `deadline` are universal first-class meters; `limits` is an extensible map of named meters (e.g. tokens, usd_opcost, or any custom unit) so the budget is not tied to a single currency or to tokens alone. A TOOL self-declares an opaque per-call contribution to named meters (via its MCP result `_meta.vap.cost`); the proxy SUMS those contributions and DENIES when ANY meter would exceed its ceiling, WITHOUT knowing what a meter means. This lets a commitment bound an aggregate side-effect (e.g. cumulative disbursed value) as a plain meter, with no protocol-level knowledge of the tool. At least one of max_calls / deadline / limits must be present.

max_calls.description: Universal ceiling on the number of tool calls in the session.

limits.description: Extensible map of named meters to their maximum allowed cumulative value. Keys are arbitrary unit names the proxy treats as opaque; well-known keys: `tokens` (LLM/tokenizer-dependent) and `usd_opcost` (monetary OPERATIONAL processing cost -- deliberately NOT bare 'usd' so it never collides with a business amount). Tools self-declare a per-call contribution per meter (result `_meta.vap.cost`, e.g. {"tokens": 50, "usd_opcost": 0.01, "disbursed_usd": 120}); the verification (LLM judge) token usage is itself billed to the `tokens` meter. Add any custom unit your tools report -- the protocol only sums and enforces ceilings.

agent_id.description: Stable agent identity. RECOMMENDED a DID (e.g. 'did:web:acme.ai:agent:billing') or a verifiable agent identifier.

auth.description: Opaque credential reference or scheme hint (e.g. 'oauth:<token-ref>'). The actual token travels in transport headers, not here.

delegated_by.description: Optional: identity of the principal that delegated authority to this agent (for delegation-chain audit).

signature.description: Optional JWS (RFC 7515) over the canonicalized message (JCS, RFC 8785), providing non-repudiation. REQUIRED when the server advertises require_signed=true.
